summary: The codec's density gradient can look navigable while semantic friction still feels heavy, so structural/projection friction must remain explicit evidence.
Narrative arc should be tested as directional trajectory and curvature, not merely a smoothed mean.
Projection loss from 768D to 8D should be visible as friction/headroom evidence before any reserved-dim or pressure feedback change.
Dynamic semantic gain or projection-loss feedback into pressure risk would change live codec/control behavior and needs V2 approval.
